title: satureja ericifolia subsp. gomerensis
intro: Satureja ericifolia subsp. gomerensis, also known as Gomer's savory or Gomer's mountain savory, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Satureja ericifolia subsp. gomerensis have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in tea, tincture, and essential oil forms, and are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
